rag_rules: Fix validator diagnostics by ensuring all AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL identifiers and types; remove invalid clauses rather than inventing new architecture.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references to AADL properties like Period, Deadline, or Priority, delete the generated clause instead of attempting to map them into AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE contracts must preserve type consistency and avoid invalid helper function usage.
AGREE assumptions and guarantees must be scoped correctly to match AADL component type or implementation boundaries.
Validator-driven repairs should only modify AGREE annex content, never alter the underlying AADL architecture.
Only use identifiers and types that are explicitly visible in the current AADL component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must follow valid AGREE syntax for expressions, constants, and equations.
Numeric variables in AGREE require numeric expressions; Boolean variables require Boolean expressions.
Helper functions in AGREE must be defined within the same annex or imported from a package.
anti_patterns: Avoid generating AGREE expressions that reference AADL property associations as variables unless the model exposes typed ports or constants for them.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by introducing new architecture or package references not present in the model.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric values in AGREE expressions without explicit scalar representation in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
Component type annexes vs. implementation annexes (not relevant to current repair task)
